articles.headline: Supreme Court Affirms Right to Clean Air as Fundamental Right, Directs National Framework
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India declared the 'Right to Clean Air' as an integral part of the fundamental Right to Life (Article 21) under the Constitution.
• The ruling mandates the Union Government to establish a comprehensive National Clean Air Framework (NCAF) within six months.
• NCAF aims to set stringent air quality standards, implement monitoring mechanisms, and enforce pollution control measures across all states.
• The judgment came in response to a Public Interest Litigation (PIL) highlighting escalating air pollution levels and their severe public health impact.
• It emphasizes inter-ministerial coordination and public participation in environmental governance.
prelimsPoints: Right to Clean Air derived from Article 21 (Right to Life).
Supreme Court's power of judicial review in environmental matters.
National Clean Air Framework (NCAF) mandated by the court.
Constitutional provisions for environmental protection (Art. 48A).
mainsPoints: Discuss the implications of declaring 'Right to Clean Air' as a fundamental right on environmental policy and governance.
Analyze the role of judicial activism in upholding environmental justice in India.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in implementing a national framework for air quality improvement, considering federal structure.
Impact on public health, economic development, and sustainable urbanization.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel striking a sound block with a backdrop of a clear sky and city skyline.
articles.trivia: India is home to 22 of the world's 30 most polluted cities, according to a 2023 report.

articles.headline: India's Q3 GDP Growth Exceeds Expectations, Driven by Manufacturing and Services
articles.explainer:
• The National Statistical Office (NSO) reported India's Gross Domestic Product (GDP) grew by 7.8% in Q3 FY25, surpassing analyst predictions.
• Manufacturing sector registered robust growth, indicating a revival in industrial activity and investment.
• Services sector, particularly financial and real estate services, continued its strong performance, contributing significantly to the overall growth.
• Government capital expenditure and private consumption were identified as key demand drivers.
• Despite global headwinds, India maintains its position as one of the fastest-growing major economies.
prelimsPoints: Q3 FY25 GDP growth rate: 7.8%.
Key drivers: Manufacturing, Services, Govt CapEx, Private Consumption.
NSO is responsible for compiling economic statistics in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to India's resilient economic growth amidst global uncertainties.
Discuss the policy implications of sustained growth in manufacturing and services sectors.
Examine the challenges India faces in maintaining high growth trajectory, such as inflation and global trade fluctuations.
articles.imageDescription: A bar graph showing economic growth with upward trends.
articles.trivia: India aims to become a $5 trillion economy by 2027.

articles.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Talks Progress Towards Critical Phase
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have entered a critical phase, with significant progress reported on key chapters.
• Both sides expressed optimism about concluding the agreement by year-end, following high-level discussions in Brussels.
• Key areas of focus include tariffs, intellectual property rights, sustainable development provisions, and market access for goods and services.
• The FTA is expected to boost bilateral trade and investment, potentially increasing India's exports to the EU by 30%.
• Challenges remain in areas like data localization and tariff harmonization for specific agricultural products.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, Regional, Global Groupings and Agreements
prelimsPoints: India-EU FTA talks are in advanced stages.
Key aspects: tariffs, IPR, sustainable development, market access.
Expected benefits: increased bilateral trade and investment.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of the India-EU FTA for India's foreign policy and trade ambitions.
Discuss the potential impact of the FTA on various sectors of the Indian economy, including manufacturing, services, and agriculture.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India in negotiating comprehensive trade agreements with major economic blocs like the EU.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union superimposed over a world map.
articles.trivia: The EU is India's third-largest trading partner, accounting for 10.8% of India's total trade in 2023-24.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Landing Technology
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully conducted a crucial landing experiment for its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator.
• The test involved releasing the RLV from an aerial platform at high altitude, followed by an autonomous descent and precise landing on a simulated runway.
• This experiment marks a significant step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle, aimed at reducing space launch costs.
• The RLV-TD program is crucial for India's future space missions, including human spaceflight (Gaganyaan) and advanced satellite deployment.
• Further tests are planned to validate other critical technologies like hypersonic flight and orbital re-entry.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- Developments and their Applications
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV-TD program aims for cost-effective space access.
Landing experiment successfully conducted for RLV.
RLV is a step towards fully reusable orbital launch vehicles.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's space program and its implications for global space access.
Analyze how RLV technology can reduce the cost of space launches and foster greater commercial participation in the space sector.
Examine the challenges and future prospects of ISRO's RLV-TD program in achieving self-reliance in space technology.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendition of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle landing on a runway.
articles.trivia: The first RLV-TD mission, HEX-01 (Hypersonic Flight Experiment-01), was successfully conducted by ISRO in 2016.

articles.headline: Parliament Passes 'National Urban Green Spaces Bill, 2025' to Enhance City Ecosystems
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Parliament passed the 'National Urban Green Spaces Bill, 2025', aiming to mandate and protect green spaces in urban areas.
• The Bill provides for the creation of a National Urban Green Spaces Authority (NUGSA) to oversee the planning, development, and maintenance of urban parks, forests, and wetlands.
• It introduces strict regulations against encroachment on designated green areas and promotes the concept of '15-minute cities' with accessible green infrastructure.
• The legislation emphasizes the ecological and public health benefits of urban greening, including air quality improvement and climate resilience.
• States will be required to formulate their own Urban Green Space Plans in alignment with the national framework.
prelimsPoints: National Urban Green Spaces Bill, 2025 passed.
NUGSA to oversee urban green spaces.
Promotes '15-minute cities' concept.
Focus on ecological and public health benefits.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the potential impact of the National Urban Green Spaces Bill, 2025, on urban development and environmental sustainability in India.
Discuss the challenges in implementing such a comprehensive framework across diverse urban landscapes and the role of local self-governments.
Examine how urban greening initiatives contribute to climate change mitigation and adaptation strategies in Indian cities.
articles.imageDescription: A lush green park in the middle of a modern city skyline.
articles.trivia: Globally, cities are responsible for 70% of greenhouse gas emissions.

articles.headline: New Tiger Reserve Declared in Eastern Ghats to Boost Conservation Efforts
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change announced the establishment of a new Tiger Reserve in the Eastern Ghats region of Andhra Pradesh.
• This declaration aims to protect the dwindling tiger population and enhance biodiversity conservation in the region, connecting existing wildlife corridors.
• The new reserve spans over 2,000 sq km, encompassing diverse forest ecosystems crucial for various endemic species.
• Local communities will be involved in co-management initiatives, promoting sustainable livelihoods and reducing human-wildlife conflict.
• This move aligns with India's commitment to Project Tiger and its goal of increasing the national tiger count.
prelimsPoints: New Tiger Reserve established in Eastern Ghats (Andhra Pradesh).
Aims to protect tigers and enhance biodiversity.
Involves local community participation.
Part of India's Project Tiger initiative.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of establishing new tiger reserves in regions like the Eastern Ghats for biodiversity conservation and ecological balance.
Analyze the challenges involved in tiger conservation, including habitat fragmentation, human-wildlife conflict, and poaching, and suggest mitigation strategies.
Evaluate the role of community participation and sustainable livelihood programs in successful wildlife conservation efforts in India.
articles.imageDescription: A majestic tiger walking through dense forest foliage.
articles.trivia: India is home to over 70% of the world's tiger population.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Cyber Suraksha Mission' to Strengthen National Cybersecurity
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched the 'Cyber Suraksha Mission', a comprehensive initiative to bolster India's cybersecurity infrastructure.
• The mission focuses on enhancing cyber resilience across critical sectors, improving threat intelligence sharing, and developing indigenous cybersecurity products.
• It includes a nationwide awareness campaign to educate citizens and organizations about cyber hygiene and best practices.
• Key components involve capacity building for cybersecurity professionals and establishing advanced cyber forensics labs.
• The initiative aims to create a secure digital ecosystem, protecting vital information infrastructure from evolving cyber threats.
prelimsPoints: 'Cyber Suraksha Mission' launched by MeitY.
Focuses on cyber resilience, threat intelligence, indigenous products.
Includes awareness campaigns and capacity building.
Aims to secure critical information infrastructure.
mainsPoints: Analyze the growing threats to India's cybersecurity landscape and the strategic importance of initiatives like the 'Cyber Suraksha Mission'.
Discuss the multi-faceted approach required to build a robust national cybersecurity framework, encompassing technology, policy, and human resources.
Examine the challenges in securing critical infrastructure and protecting citizen data in an increasingly digitized economy.
articles.imageDescription: A digital padlock icon overlaid on a map of India, representing cybersecurity.
articles.trivia: India ranks among the top countries targeted by cyberattacks globally.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Challenges to Internal Security through Communication Networks

articles.headline: National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 Implementation Review Highlights Progress, Challenges
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released its annual review of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation, showcasing significant strides in foundational literacy and numeracy.
• The report highlighted successful integration of vocational education and multidisciplinary learning in higher education institutions.
• Challenges persist in teacher training, digital infrastructure in rural areas, and achieving equitable access to quality education.
• The review emphasized the need for increased public-private partnerships and innovative financing models to meet NEP's ambitious goals.
• Recommendations include accelerating the development of the National Curriculum Framework and enhancing capacity building for educators.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 implementation reviewed by Ministry of Education.
Progress in foundational literacy, vocational education.
Challenges: teacher training, digital infrastructure, equitable access.
Recommendations: PPPs, NCF development, educator capacity building.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the progress and challenges in the implementation of National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 across different levels of education.
Discuss how NEP 2020 aims to address issues of equity, access, and quality in the Indian education system.
Suggest innovative strategies to overcome the persistent challenges identified in the NEP implementation review, particularly in rural and underserved areas.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting, symbolizing education reform.
articles.trivia: NEP 2020 replaced the 34-year-old National Policy on Education (1986).

articles.headline: RBI Introduces New Digital Currency Pilot for Wholesale Segment
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) announced the launch of a new pilot program for its Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC), focusing on the wholesale segment.
• This initiative aims to streamline interbank settlements, reduce transaction costs, and enhance efficiency in the financial markets.
• The pilot involves a consortium of major public and private sector banks, testing various use cases for the digital Rupee in wholesale transactions.
• RBI stated that insights from this pilot would inform the eventual broader rollout of both wholesale and retail CBDCs.
• The move underscores India's commitment to leveraging digital innovation in its financial infrastructure while ensuring monetary stability.
prelimsPoints: RBI launches new CBDC pilot for wholesale segment.
Aims to streamline interbank settlements and reduce costs.
Involves major public and private sector banks.
Insights to inform broader CBDC rollout.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind the Reserve Bank of India's introduction of a Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) for the wholesale segment and its potential benefits for the financial system.
Analyze the challenges and risks associated with implementing a CBDC, including cybersecurity, financial stability, and privacy concerns.
Compare and contrast the potential impact of wholesale CBDC with retail CBDC on India's digital economy and financial inclusion goals.
articles.imageDescription: A digital representation of currency notes with the RBI logo.
articles.trivia: India was among the first major economies to announce plans for a CBDC.

articles.headline: India Pledges Support for Global South Climate Adaptation Fund at UN Summit
articles.explainer:
• India reiterated its commitment to climate action by pledging significant support for a new 'Global South Climate Adaptation Fund' at a UN-backed summit.
• The fund aims to provide financial and technical assistance to developing nations disproportionately affected by climate change impacts.
• India emphasized the principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC) in climate finance.
• The initiative seeks to bridge the gap in climate adaptation funding, which has historically lagged behind mitigation efforts.
• India called for greater transparency and accountability in climate finance mechanisms to ensure equitable distribution.
keyTerms: Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC)
prelimsPoints: India pledged support for 'Global South Climate Adaptation Fund'.
Fund aims to assist developing nations with climate impacts.
India reiterated CBDR-RC principle.
Focus on bridging climate adaptation funding gap.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's role and stance on climate finance, particularly regarding the 'Global South Climate Adaptation Fund', in the context of international climate negotiations.
Discuss the significance of climate adaptation for developing countries and the challenges in mobilizing adequate finance for these initiatives.
Examine the principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC) and its relevance in contemporary climate diplomacy.
articles.imageDescription: A globe with hands reaching out, symbolizing global cooperation on climate change.
articles.trivia: India is the third-largest emitter of greenhouse gases globally but has one of the lowest per capita emissions.

articles.headline: Panel Recommends Reforms for Lokpal and Lokayukta Act to Enhance Transparency
articles.explainer:
• A high-level parliamentary panel submitted its recommendations for amendments to the Lokpal and Lokayukta Act, 2013, aiming to strengthen its efficacy and transparency.
• Key recommendations include expanding the Lokpal's jurisdiction to cover all public servants, irrespective of rank, and simplifying the complaint filing process.
• The panel also suggested measures to ensure greater financial and administrative autonomy for both Lokpal and state Lokayuktas.
• Emphasis was placed on protecting whistleblowers and ensuring timely disposal of corruption cases.
• The proposed reforms seek to address existing operational bottlenecks and enhance public trust in anti-corruption institutions.
prelimsPoints: Panel recommended amendments to Lokpal and Lokayukta Act, 2013.
Key recommendations: expand jurisdiction, simplify complaint process, greater autonomy.
Focus on whistleblower protection and timely case disposal.
Aims to enhance transparency and efficacy.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the effectiveness of the Lokpal and Lokayukta institutions in combating corruption in India since their inception.
Discuss the significance of the proposed reforms to the Lokpal and Lokayukta Act, 2013, in strengthening accountability and transparency in public administration.
Examine the challenges in implementing such anti-corruption mechanisms and suggest further measures to ensure their independence and operational efficiency.
articles.imageDescription: A weighing scale balancing justice and accountability symbols.
articles.trivia: The concept of an ombudsman, similar to Lokpal, originated in Sweden in 1809.

articles.headline: AI-Powered Diagnostic Tool for Early Cancer Detection Launched in Public Hospitals
articles.explainer:
• A new Artificial Intelligence (AI)-powered diagnostic tool, developed by Indian researchers, has been launched in select public hospitals for early cancer detection.
• The tool utilizes machine learning algorithms to analyze medical images (e.g., mammograms, biopsies) with high accuracy, assisting oncologists in faster diagnosis.
• It aims to address the challenge of delayed cancer diagnosis, particularly in underserved areas with limited access to specialized medical expertise.
• The initiative is part of the National Health Mission's focus on leveraging technology for improved healthcare outcomes.
• Initial trials showed a significant reduction in diagnostic time and an increase in early-stage detection rates, improving patient prognosis.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- Developments and their Applications
prelimsPoints: AI-powered tool launched for early cancer detection in public hospitals.
Uses machine learning to analyze medical images.
Aims to reduce diagnostic time and improve early detection.
Part of National Health Mission initiatives.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of Artificial Intelligence in revolutionizing healthcare delivery, particularly in areas like disease diagnosis and treatment in India.
Analyze the ethical considerations and challenges associated with the widespread adoption of AI in medical diagnostics, including data privacy and algorithmic bias.
Examine how technological advancements, such as AI, can bridge the healthcare access gap and improve health outcomes in rural and remote areas of India.
articles.imageDescription: A doctor interacting with a holographic display showing medical data and an AI interface.
articles.trivia: Globally, cancer is the second leading cause of death.

articles.headline: Centre Unveils 'Ganga Rejuvenation Phase III' with Focus on Tributary Health
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti launched 'Ganga Rejuvenation Phase III', an ambitious program building on previous efforts to clean and conserve the River Ganga.
• This phase places a strong emphasis on the health of Ganga's tributaries, recognizing their crucial role in the overall river ecosystem.
• Key components include enhanced wastewater treatment infrastructure along tributaries, afforestation drives in catchment areas, and promotion of sustainable agricultural practices.
• The program also involves greater public participation and inter-state cooperation for effective water resource management.
• Aims to achieve 'Nirmal Ganga' (Clean Ganga) and 'Aviral Ganga' (Flowing Ganga) by 2030, ensuring ecological flow and biodiversity.
prelimsPoints: 'Ganga Rejuvenation Phase III' launched by Ministry of Jal Shakti.
Focus on health of Ganga's tributaries.
Components: wastewater treatment, afforestation, sustainable agriculture.
Aims for 'Nirmal Ganga' and 'Aviral Ganga' by 2030.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the progress and challenges of the 'Namami Gange' program and discuss how 'Ganga Rejuvenation Phase III' aims to address previous shortcomings.
Analyze the ecological and socio-economic significance of maintaining the health of river tributaries for the overall river basin ecosystem.
Discuss the importance of inter-state cooperation and public participation in large-scale river conservation projects in India.
articles.imageDescription: A clean section of the Ganga river with lush banks, symbolizing rejuvenation.
articles.trivia: The Ganga river basin is the most populous river basin in the world.

articles.headline: PLI Scheme for Advanced Battery Manufacturing Attracts Major Investment
articles.explainer:
• The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Manufacturing has successfully attracted significant domestic and international investments.
• Several major players announced plans to set up gigafactories in India, boosting the country's capacity for electric vehicle (EV) battery production.
• The scheme aims to reduce India's reliance on battery imports, foster local manufacturing, and position India as a global hub for ACC battery technology.
• This surge in investment is critical for achieving India's ambitious electric mobility targets and reducing carbon emissions from the transport sector.
• The government expects the scheme to generate thousands of direct and indirect jobs and create a robust ecosystem for battery research and development.
prelimsPoints: PLI scheme for ACC Battery Manufacturing attracts significant investment.
Aims to boost EV battery production and reduce imports.
Supports India's electric mobility targets.
Expected to create jobs and R&D ecosystem.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the effectiveness of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme in promoting domestic manufacturing and attracting foreign investment in key strategic sectors like Advanced Chemistry Cell batteries.
Discuss the role of indigenous battery manufacturing in accelerating India's transition to electric mobility and achieving its climate change mitigation goals.
Analyze the economic and environmental benefits, as well as the challenges, associated with establishing a robust EV battery manufacturing ecosystem in India.
articles.imageDescription: A schematic of a battery cell with an arrow indicating production.
articles.trivia: Lithium-ion batteries are currently the most common type of ACC battery used in EVs.

articles.headline: Campaign Launched to Combat Digital Divide in Rural Healthcare Access
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, in collaboration with NITI Aayog, launched a nationwide campaign to bridge the digital divide in rural healthcare access.
• The initiative focuses on enhancing digital literacy among rural health workers and patients, promoting telemedicine services, and deploying digital health records systems.
• It aims to ensure equitable access to quality healthcare services by leveraging technology, especially in remote and underserved areas.
• Training modules for ASHA workers and community health officers are being developed to effectively utilize digital tools for primary healthcare delivery.
• The campaign also seeks to improve internet connectivity and provide affordable digital devices in rural health centers.
prelimsPoints: Campaign launched by Ministry of Health and NITI Aayog.
Aims to bridge digital divide in rural healthcare.
Focus on digital literacy, telemedicine, DHR.
Training for ASHA workers and improved connectivity.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges posed by the digital divide in achieving equitable healthcare access in rural India and how the new campaign aims to address them.
Analyze the role of telemedicine and digital health records in transforming primary healthcare delivery and improving health outcomes in remote areas.
Examine the importance of community health workers, like ASHA, in leveraging digital tools for public health initiatives and last-mile connectivity.
articles.imageDescription: A rural health worker using a tablet to connect with a doctor, symbolizing telemedicine.
articles.trivia: Only about 40% of India's rural population has access to broadband internet.

articles.headline: G20 Finance Ministers Meet to Discuss Global Economic Stability and Debt Restructuring
articles.explainer:
• Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors from G20 nations convened for a crucial meeting to address pressing global economic challenges.
• Discussions focused on strategies to ensure global economic stability amidst geopolitical uncertainties and persistent inflation pressures.
• A significant agenda item was debt restructuring for vulnerable low-income countries, with calls for greater creditor coordination and transparency.
• India, as a prominent voice for the Global South, advocated for equitable access to finance and resilient supply chains.
• The meeting concluded with a commitment to strengthen multilateral cooperation and enhance coordination on fiscal and monetary policies.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, Regional, Global Groupings and Agreements
prelimsPoints: G20 Finance Ministers met to discuss global economic stability.
Key topics: debt restructuring for low-income countries, inflation.
India advocated for equitable finance and resilient supply chains.
Commitment to multilateral cooperation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of the G20 in addressing global economic challenges and its effectiveness in fostering multilateral cooperation on financial and monetary policies.
Discuss the growing concern of debt distress in vulnerable economies and the importance of coordinated debt restructuring initiatives by major creditors.
Examine India's position and contributions in the G20 forum, particularly in advocating for the interests of the Global South and promoting inclusive economic growth.
articles.imageDescription: A conference table with G20 country flags, symbolizing international economic discussions.
articles.trivia: The G20 represents about two-thirds of the world's population, 85% of global GDP, and over 75% of global trade.

articles.headline: India-Sri Lanka Hold High-Level Maritime Security Dialogue
articles.explainer:
• India and Sri Lanka conducted a high-level maritime security dialogue aimed at enhancing cooperation in the Indian Ocean Region.
• Discussions focused on joint patrolling, intelligence sharing, capacity building for maritime surveillance, and combating transnational crimes like drug trafficking and illegal fishing.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and emphasized mutual security interests.
• The dialogue also explored avenues for collaboration in marine resource management and environmental protection in shared waters.
• This engagement underscores the growing strategic partnership between the two littoral states.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, Regional, Global Groupings and Agreements
Security Challenges and their Management in Border Areas
prelimsPoints: India and Sri Lanka held maritime security dialogue.
Focus on joint patrolling, intelligence sharing, capacity building.
Commitment to free and open Indo-Pacific.
Collaboration in marine resource management.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of maritime security cooperation between India and Sri Lanka for regional stability in the Indian Ocean Region.
Discuss the common maritime challenges faced by India and Sri Lanka, such as illegal fishing, drug trafficking, and external naval presence, and how bilateral cooperation can address them.
Examine India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy and its implications for strengthening ties with littoral states like Sri Lanka.
articles.imageDescription: Naval ships of India and Sri Lanka sailing together on the ocean.
articles.trivia: The Palk Strait separates India from Sri Lanka.

articles.headline: India's Solar Energy Capacity Crosses 100 GW Mark, Targets 500 GW by 2030
articles.explainer:
• India announced a significant milestone, with its installed solar energy capacity crossing the 100 GW (gigawatt) mark.
• This achievement underscores the country's rapid progress in renewable energy adoption and commitment to climate goals.
• The growth is attributed to supportive government policies, declining solar panel costs, and increased private sector investment.
• India is now firmly on track to achieve its ambitious target of 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.
• Challenges remain in grid integration, energy storage, and ensuring a stable supply to meet peak demand.
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: India's solar energy capacity crosses 100 GW.
Target: 500 GW renewable energy by 2030.
Growth driven by government policies and private investment.
Challenges: grid integration, storage.
mainsPoints: Evaluate India's progress in expanding its renewable energy capacity, particularly solar energy, and its implications for energy security and climate change mitigation.
Discuss the policy frameworks and incentives that have contributed to the rapid growth of solar power in India.
Examine the challenges associated with integrating large-scale intermittent renewable energy sources into the national grid and suggest solutions for energy storage and grid stability.
articles.imageDescription: A vast field of solar panels basking under the sun.
articles.trivia: The world's largest solar power plant, Bhadla Solar Park, is located in Rajasthan, India.

articles.headline: UN Report Highlights Mixed Progress in Gender Equality in India
articles.explainer:
• A recent UN report on gender equality globally highlighted mixed progress in India, noting significant advancements in political representation but persistent gaps in economic participation.
• The report praised India's efforts in increasing women's political participation at local governance levels and improving access to education for girls.
• However, it pointed out continued challenges in closing the gender pay gap, reducing female labor force participation, and addressing gender-based violence.
• Recommendations included strengthening legal frameworks, investing in women's economic empowerment programs, and challenging societal norms.
• The report emphasized that achieving full gender equality is crucial for sustainable development and economic growth in India.
gsSubTopics: Role of Women and Women's Organization
Issues Relating to Development and Management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: UN report on gender equality in India shows mixed progress.
Advancements in political representation, girls' education.
Challenges in gender pay gap, FLFP, gender-based violence.
Recommendations: legal frameworks, economic empowerment, societal norms.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the progress and persistent challenges in achieving gender equality in India, drawing insights from the recent UN report.
Discuss the multi-dimensional approach required to empower women, encompassing political, economic, and social dimensions, and the role of government policies in this regard.
Examine how societal norms and cultural practices impact women's participation in the economy and public life, and suggest strategies for transformative change.
articles.imageDescription: A diverse group of women standing together, symbolizing empowerment.
articles.trivia: The UN's Sustainable Development Goal 5 aims to achieve gender equality and empower all women and girls.

highlights: Supreme Court declares 'Right to Clean Air' a fundamental right under Article 21, mandates National Clean Air Framework.
India's Q3 GDP growth hits 7.8%, driven by strong manufacturing and services sectors, exceeding expectations.
ISRO successfully tests Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) landing technology, a major step for cost-effective space access.
Parliament passes 'National Urban Green Spaces Bill, 2025' to protect and enhance urban ecosystems nationwide.
India's solar energy capacity crosses 100 GW mark, putting the nation on track for 500 GW renewable energy by 2030.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent Supreme Court ruling on the 'Right to Clean Air', consider the following statements:
1. The Supreme Court declared 'Right to Clean Air' as an integral part of Article 19 (Right to Freedom).
2. The ruling mandates the Union Government to establish a National Clean Air Framework (NCAF).
3. The NCAF aims to set stringent air quality standards and enforce pollution control measures.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The Supreme Court declared 'Right to Clean Air' as an integral part of Article 21 (Right to Life), not Article 19. Statements 2 and 3 are correct, as the ruling mandates the establishment of a National Clean Air Framework (NCAF) for setting air quality standards and enforcing pollution control measures.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for compiling India's Gross Domestic Product (GDP) data?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The National Statistical Office (NSO), under the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation, is responsible for compiling and releasing India's GDP data.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program:
1. The RLV-TD program aims to develop technologies for fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicles.
2. The recent test involved an autonomous landing of the RLV from an aerial platform.
3. RLV technology is primarily intended for inter-planetary missions and not for human spaceflight.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct. The RLV-TD program is indeed aimed at developing fully reusable orbital launch vehicles and the recent test involved an autonomous landing. Statement 3 is incorrect; RLV technology is crucial for reducing space launch costs for various missions, including human spaceflight like Gaganyaan, and advanced satellite deployment.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'Cyber Suraksha Mission' recently launched by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) primarily focuses on:
options: (a) Promoting digital literacy in rural areas for e-governance services.
(b) Strengthening India's cybersecurity infrastructure and resilience.
(c) Developing indigenous hardware for telecommunications networks.
(d) Regulating social media platforms to combat misinformation.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Strengthening India's cybersecurity infrastructure and resilience.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'Cyber Suraksha Mission' aims to bolster India's cybersecurity infrastructure, enhance cyber resilience across critical sectors, improve threat intelligence sharing, and develop indigenous cybersecurity products.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following statements about India's solar energy capacity is correct?
options: (a) India's total installed solar energy capacity is less than 50 GW.
(b) India aims to achieve 100 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.
(c) India's installed solar energy capacity has recently crossed the 100 GW mark.
(d) India is primarily focusing on thermal energy for its future energy needs.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) India's installed solar energy capacity has recently crossed the 100 GW mark.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The article states that India's installed solar energy capacity has crossed the 100 GW mark. Option (a) is incorrect. Option (b) is incorrect as India aims for 500 GW of *renewable* energy capacity by 2030, not just 100 GW. Option (d) is incorrect as India is significantly expanding its renewable energy sector.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's declaration of the 'Right to Clean Air' as a fundamental right marks a significant milestone in environmental jurisprudence. Discuss the implications of this ruling on environmental policy-making and governance in India, and analyze the challenges in its effective implementation. (15 marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Fundamental Rights, Government Policies and Interventions; GS3: Environmental Protection, Conservation
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the SC ruling and its basis (Art 21). Discuss positive implications like stronger policy push, judicial oversight, public awareness. Analyze challenges such as federal structure, enforcement mechanisms, economic costs, and public participation. Conclude with a way forward for effective implementation.

mainsMocks.question: Evaluate the strategic and economic significance of the India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) for India's trade ambitions and geopolitical standing. What are the key areas of mutual benefit and the potential hurdles that need to be addressed for a successful conclusion? (15 marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Bilateral, Regional, Global Groupings and Agreements involving India; GS3: Indian Economy, International Trade
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by outlining the current status of India-EU trade. Discuss strategic significance (diversification, geopolitical balance) and economic benefits (market access, investment, technology transfer). Identify mutual benefit areas (tariffs, IPR). Highlight hurdles (data localization, specific sector tariffs) and suggest solutions for successful conclusion.

mainsMocks.question: India's journey towards a cleaner energy future is marked by ambitious targets and significant progress. Critically analyze the factors contributing to India's recent milestone in solar energy capacity and discuss the challenges associated with large-scale integration of renewable energy into the national grid. (10 marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Energy, Infrastructure, Environmental Pollution and Degradation
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce India's renewable energy goals and the solar capacity milestone. Discuss contributing factors (government policies, PLI schemes, cost reduction, private investment). Analyze challenges like grid stability, intermittency, energy storage, land acquisition, and financing. Conclude with steps to overcome these challenges.

quickBytes.text: Article 21 of the Indian Constitution guarantees the Right to Life and Personal Liberty.
The National Statistical Office (NSO) is responsible for compiling India's national accounts and industrial statistics.
The European Union (EU) is a political and economic union of 27 member states.
ISRO's Gaganyaan mission aims to demonstrate India's human spaceflight capability by 2025.
Project Tiger, launched in 1973, is a wildlife conservation project in India which aims at conserving tigers.
The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) is responsible for policy matters relating to IT.
The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 replaced the 34-year-old National Policy on Education (1986).
Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) is a digital form of a country's fiat currency, issued by the central bank.
The G20 is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union (EU).
The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme aims to boost domestic manufacturing and exports.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 Implementation Review Highlights Progress, Challenges
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released its annual review of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation, showcasing significant strides in foundational literacy and numeracy.
• The report highlighted successful integration of vocational education and multidisciplinary learning in higher education institutions.
• Challenges persist in teacher training, digital infrastructure in rural areas, and achieving equitable access to quality education.
• The review emphasized the need for increased public-private partnerships and innovative financing models to meet NEP's ambitious goals.
• Recommendations include accelerating the development of the National Curriculum Framework and enhancing capacity building for educators.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 implementation reviewed by Ministry of Education.
Progress in foundational literacy, vocational education.
Challenges: teacher training, digital infrastructure, equitable access.
Recommendations: PPPs, NCF development, educator capacity building.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the progress and challenges in the implementation of National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 across different levels of education.
Discuss how NEP 2020 aims to address issues of equity, access, and quality in the Indian education system.
Suggest innovative strategies to overcome the persistent challenges identified in the NEP implementation review, particularly in rural and underserved areas.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting, symbolizing education reform.
GS2.trivia: NEP 2020 replaced the 34-year-old National Policy on Education (1986).

GS2.headline: Panel Recommends Reforms for Lokpal and Lokayukta Act to Enhance Transparency
GS2.explainer:
• A high-level parliamentary panel submitted its recommendations for amendments to the Lokpal and Lokayukta Act, 2013, aiming to strengthen its efficacy and transparency.
• Key recommendations include expanding the Lokpal's jurisdiction to cover all public servants, irrespective of rank, and simplifying the complaint filing process.
• The panel also suggested measures to ensure greater financial and administrative autonomy for both Lokpal and state Lokayuktas.
• Emphasis was placed on protecting whistleblowers and ensuring timely disposal of corruption cases.
• The proposed reforms seek to address existing operational bottlenecks and enhance public trust in anti-corruption institutions.
prelimsPoints: Panel recommended amendments to Lokpal and Lokayukta Act, 2013.
Key recommendations: expand jurisdiction, simplify complaint process, greater autonomy.
Focus on whistleblower protection and timely case disposal.
Aims to enhance transparency and efficacy.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the effectiveness of the Lokpal and Lokayukta institutions in combating corruption in India since their inception.
Discuss the significance of the proposed reforms to the Lokpal and Lokayukta Act, 2013, in strengthening accountability and transparency in public administration.
Examine the challenges in implementing such anti-corruption mechanisms and suggest further measures to ensure their independence and operational efficiency.
GS2.imageDescription: A weighing scale balancing justice and accountability symbols.
GS2.trivia: The concept of an ombudsman, similar to Lokpal, originated in Sweden in 1809.

GS3.headline: India's Q3 GDP Growth Exceeds Expectations, Driven by Manufacturing and Services
GS3.explainer:
• The National Statistical Office (NSO) reported India's Gross Domestic Product (GDP) grew by 7.8% in Q3 FY25, surpassing analyst predictions.
• Manufacturing sector registered robust growth, indicating a revival in industrial activity and investment.
• Services sector, particularly financial and real estate services, continued its strong performance, contributing significantly to the overall growth.
• Government capital expenditure and private consumption were identified as key demand drivers.
• Despite global headwinds, India maintains its position as one of the fastest-growing major economies.
prelimsPoints: Q3 FY25 GDP growth rate: 7.8%.
Key drivers: Manufacturing, Services, Govt CapEx, Private Consumption.
NSO is responsible for compiling economic statistics in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to India's resilient economic growth amidst global uncertainties.
Discuss the policy implications of sustained growth in manufacturing and services sectors.
Examine the challenges India faces in maintaining high growth trajectory, such as inflation and global trade fluctuations.
GS3.imageDescription: A bar graph showing economic growth with upward trends.
GS3.trivia: India aims to become a $5 trillion economy by 2027.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Landing Technology
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully conducted a crucial landing experiment for its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator.
• The test involved releasing the RLV from an aerial platform at high altitude, followed by an autonomous descent and precise landing on a simulated runway.
• This experiment marks a significant step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle, aimed at reducing space launch costs.
• The RLV-TD program is crucial for India's future space missions, including human spaceflight (Gaganyaan) and advanced satellite deployment.
• Further tests are planned to validate other critical technologies like hypersonic flight and orbital re-entry.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- Developments and their Applications
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV-TD program aims for cost-effective space access.
Landing experiment successfully conducted for RLV.
RLV is a step towards fully reusable orbital launch vehicles.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's space program and its implications for global space access.
Analyze how RLV technology can reduce the cost of space launches and foster greater commercial participation in the space sector.
Examine the challenges and future prospects of ISRO's RLV-TD program in achieving self-reliance in space technology.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendition of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle landing on a runway.
GS3.trivia: The first RLV-TD mission, HEX-01 (Hypersonic Flight Experiment-01), was successfully conducted by ISRO in 2016.

GS3.headline: New Tiger Reserve Declared in Eastern Ghats to Boost Conservation Efforts
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change announced the establishment of a new Tiger Reserve in the Eastern Ghats region of Andhra Pradesh.
• This declaration aims to protect the dwindling tiger population and enhance biodiversity conservation in the region, connecting existing wildlife corridors.
• The new reserve spans over 2,000 sq km, encompassing diverse forest ecosystems crucial for various endemic species.
• Local communities will be involved in co-management initiatives, promoting sustainable livelihoods and reducing human-wildlife conflict.
• This move aligns with India's commitment to Project Tiger and its goal of increasing the national tiger count.
prelimsPoints: New Tiger Reserve established in Eastern Ghats (Andhra Pradesh).
Aims to protect tigers and enhance biodiversity.
Involves local community participation.
Part of India's Project Tiger initiative.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of establishing new tiger reserves in regions like the Eastern Ghats for biodiversity conservation and ecological balance.
Analyze the challenges involved in tiger conservation, including habitat fragmentation, human-wildlife conflict, and poaching, and suggest mitigation strategies.
Evaluate the role of community participation and sustainable livelihood programs in successful wildlife conservation efforts in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A majestic tiger walking through dense forest foliage.
GS3.trivia: India is home to over 70% of the world's tiger population.

GS3.headline: Government Launches 'Cyber Suraksha Mission' to Strengthen National Cybersecurity
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched the 'Cyber Suraksha Mission', a comprehensive initiative to bolster India's cybersecurity infrastructure.
• The mission focuses on enhancing cyber resilience across critical sectors, improving threat intelligence sharing, and developing indigenous cybersecurity products.
• It includes a nationwide awareness campaign to educate citizens and organizations about cyber hygiene and best practices.
• Key components involve capacity building for cybersecurity professionals and establishing advanced cyber forensics labs.
• The initiative aims to create a secure digital ecosystem, protecting vital information infrastructure from evolving cyber threats.
prelimsPoints: 'Cyber Suraksha Mission' launched by MeitY.
Focuses on cyber resilience, threat intelligence, indigenous products.
Includes awareness campaigns and capacity building.
Aims to secure critical information infrastructure.
mainsPoints: Analyze the growing threats to India's cybersecurity landscape and the strategic importance of initiatives like the 'Cyber Suraksha Mission'.
Discuss the multi-faceted approach required to build a robust national cybersecurity framework, encompassing technology, policy, and human resources.
Examine the challenges in securing critical infrastructure and protecting citizen data in an increasingly digitized economy.
GS3.imageDescription: A digital padlock icon overlaid on a map of India, representing cybersecurity.
GS3.trivia: India ranks among the top countries targeted by cyberattacks globally.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Challenges to Internal Security through Communication Networks

GS3.headline: RBI Introduces New Digital Currency Pilot for Wholesale Segment
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) announced the launch of a new pilot program for its Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC), focusing on the wholesale segment.
• This initiative aims to streamline interbank settlements, reduce transaction costs, and enhance efficiency in the financial markets.
• The pilot involves a consortium of major public and private sector banks, testing various use cases for the digital Rupee in wholesale transactions.
• RBI stated that insights from this pilot would inform the eventual broader rollout of both wholesale and retail CBDCs.
• The move underscores India's commitment to leveraging digital innovation in its financial infrastructure while ensuring monetary stability.
prelimsPoints: RBI launches new CBDC pilot for wholesale segment.
Aims to streamline interbank settlements and reduce costs.
Involves major public and private sector banks.
Insights to inform broader CBDC rollout.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind the Reserve Bank of India's introduction of a Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) for the wholesale segment and its potential benefits for the financial system.
Analyze the challenges and risks associated with implementing a CBDC, including cybersecurity, financial stability, and privacy concerns.
Compare and contrast the potential impact of wholesale CBDC with retail CBDC on India's digital economy and financial inclusion goals.
GS3.imageDescription: A digital representation of currency notes with the RBI logo.
GS3.trivia: India was among the first major economies to announce plans for a CBDC.

GS3.headline: Centre Unveils 'Ganga Rejuvenation Phase III' with Focus on Tributary Health
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti launched 'Ganga Rejuvenation Phase III', an ambitious program building on previous efforts to clean and conserve the River Ganga.
• This phase places a strong emphasis on the health of Ganga's tributaries, recognizing their crucial role in the overall river ecosystem.
• Key components include enhanced wastewater treatment infrastructure along tributaries, afforestation drives in catchment areas, and promotion of sustainable agricultural practices.
• The program also involves greater public participation and inter-state cooperation for effective water resource management.
• Aims to achieve 'Nirmal Ganga' (Clean Ganga) and 'Aviral Ganga' (Flowing Ganga) by 2030, ensuring ecological flow and biodiversity.
prelimsPoints: 'Ganga Rejuvenation Phase III' launched by Ministry of Jal Shakti.
Focus on health of Ganga's tributaries.
Components: wastewater treatment, afforestation, sustainable agriculture.
Aims for 'Nirmal Ganga' and 'Aviral Ganga' by 2030.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the progress and challenges of the 'Namami Gange' program and discuss how 'Ganga Rejuvenation Phase III' aims to address previous shortcomings.
Analyze the ecological and socio-economic significance of maintaining the health of river tributaries for the overall river basin ecosystem.
Discuss the importance of inter-state cooperation and public participation in large-scale river conservation projects in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A clean section of the Ganga river with lush banks, symbolizing rejuvenation.
GS3.trivia: The Ganga river basin is the most populous river basin in the world.

GS3.headline: PLI Scheme for Advanced Battery Manufacturing Attracts Major Investment
GS3.explainer:
• The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Manufacturing has successfully attracted significant domestic and international investments.
• Several major players announced plans to set up gigafactories in India, boosting the country's capacity for electric vehicle (EV) battery production.
• The scheme aims to reduce India's reliance on battery imports, foster local manufacturing, and position India as a global hub for ACC battery technology.
• This surge in investment is critical for achieving India's ambitious electric mobility targets and reducing carbon emissions from the transport sector.
• The government expects the scheme to generate thousands of direct and indirect jobs and create a robust ecosystem for battery research and development.
prelimsPoints: PLI scheme for ACC Battery Manufacturing attracts significant investment.
Aims to boost EV battery production and reduce imports.
Supports India's electric mobility targets.
Expected to create jobs and R&D ecosystem.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the effectiveness of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme in promoting domestic manufacturing and attracting foreign investment in key strategic sectors like Advanced Chemistry Cell batteries.
Discuss the role of indigenous battery manufacturing in accelerating India's transition to electric mobility and achieving its climate change mitigation goals.
Analyze the economic and environmental benefits, as well as the challenges, associated with establishing a robust EV battery manufacturing ecosystem in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A schematic of a battery cell with an arrow indicating production.
GS3.trivia: Lithium-ion batteries are currently the most common type of ACC battery used in EVs.

GS3.headline: India's Solar Energy Capacity Crosses 100 GW Mark, Targets 500 GW by 2030
GS3.explainer:
• India announced a significant milestone, with its installed solar energy capacity crossing the 100 GW (gigawatt) mark.
• This achievement underscores the country's rapid progress in renewable energy adoption and commitment to climate goals.
• The growth is attributed to supportive government policies, declining solar panel costs, and increased private sector investment.
• India is now firmly on track to achieve its ambitious target of 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.
• Challenges remain in grid integration, energy storage, and ensuring a stable supply to meet peak demand.
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: India's solar energy capacity crosses 100 GW.
Target: 500 GW renewable energy by 2030.
Growth driven by government policies and private investment.
Challenges: grid integration, storage.
mainsPoints: Evaluate India's progress in expanding its renewable energy capacity, particularly solar energy, and its implications for energy security and climate change mitigation.
Discuss the policy frameworks and incentives that have contributed to the rapid growth of solar power in India.
Examine the challenges associated with integrating large-scale intermittent renewable energy sources into the national grid and suggest solutions for energy storage and grid stability.
GS3.imageDescription: A vast field of solar panels basking under the sun.
GS3.trivia: The world's largest solar power plant, Bhadla Solar Park, is located in Rajasthan, India.

sources: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
